heading: Assistant Secretaries; Deputy Assistant Secretaries
label: 308 Assistant Secretaries; Deputy Assistant Secretaries

children.text: There shall be in the Department not more than seven Assistant Secretaries.
Except as provided in paragraph (3), each Assistant Secretary appointed under paragraph (1) shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.label: A
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for Management.

children.label: B
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for Human Resources and Administration.

children.label: C
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for Public and Intergovernmental Affairs.

children.label: D
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for Operations, Security, and Preparedness.
Budgetary and financial functions.
Personnel management and labor relations functions.
Planning, studies, and evaluations.
Management, productivity, and logistic support functions.
Information management functions as required by .section 3506 of title 44
Capital facilities and real property program functions.
Equal opportunity functions.
Functions regarding the investigation of complaints of employment discrimination within the Department.
Functions regarding intergovernmental, public, and consumer information and affairs.
Procurement functions.
Operations, preparedness, security, and law enforcement functions.
The functions set forth in .section 323(c) of this title

children.label: c
children.text: Whenever the President nominates an individual for appointment as an Assistant Secretary, the President shall include in the communication to the Senate of the nomination a statement of the particular functions of the Department specified in subsection (b), and any other functions of the Department, the individual will exercise upon taking office.

There shall be in the Department such number of Deputy Assistant Secretaries, not exceeding 19, as the Secretary may determine. Each Deputy Assistant Secretary shall be appointed by the Secretary and shall perform such functions as the Secretary prescribes.

children.label: A
children.text: of a confidential, policy-determining, policy-making, or policy-advocating character;

children.label: B
children.text: in which such individual served as a noncareer appointee in the Senior Executive Service, as such term is defined in ; orsection 3132(a)(7) of title 5

children.label: C
children.text: to which such individual was appointed by the President.
